title: Synergistic effects of salt-tolerant lactic acid bacteria on flavour profiles, bioactive compounds and microbial communities in traditional earthen-jar fermented fruit radish paocai.
author_string: Hu, Yingxi, Fan, Zixi, Sun, Qiao, Chen, Wanying, Zhong, Maoling et al.
abstract: This study investigated the synergistic effects of three functionally diverse, salt-tolerant lactic acid bacteria on the physicochemical properties, bioactive compounds, antioxidant activity, metabolic profiles and microbial community composition during traditional earthen-jar fermentation of novel fruit radish paocai. The results indicated that paocai co-fermented with tri-strain attained a pH of 3.50 ± 0.1 by day 7 with remarkably accelerated acidification and a minimum nitrite content of 6.05 ± 0.11 mg/kg, and enhanced sensory attributes compared with other experimental groups (P < 0.05). Moreover, this group showed considerably elevated concentrations of total phenols (0.70 ± 0.15 g/kg), flavonoids (0.87 ± 0.05 g/kg) and anthocyanins (72.72 ± 0.05 mg/kg), along with markedly improved antioxidant activity (P < 0.01). The metabolic profile exhibited elevated lactic acid, glyceraldehyde, dihydroxyacetone, and maltose, yet reduced taxifolin, acacetin, lysophosphatidylcholine, leucine, and isoleucine in tri-strain co-fermentation, with volatile flavours showing heightened fruity compounds/ethyl caprylate and diminished sulphur compounds. These differential metabolites were principally associated with propanoate and pyruvate metabolism, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, ATP-binding cassette transporters and protein digestion/absorption pathways‌. The tri-strain co-fermentation demonstrated significantly lower alpha diversity yet a more stable microbial community, which was dominated by Lactiplantibacillus (>85 %), than that of the natural fermentation group. A markedly positive correlation existed between Lactiplantibacillus abundance and lactic acid yield, whereas negative correlations with malodorous compounds were evident (|r| > 0.6, P < 0.05). Three salt-tolerant strains synergistically enhanced fermentation by inhibiting Enterobacter metabolism while complementing Weissella and Leuconostoc functionally, thus notably improving the flavour quality, bioactivity and safety of fruit radish paocai.
